answer:
In Johann Gottlieb Fichte's *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens* (1793), the German term *Volk* (or plural *Völker*) appears several times in the original text, most prominently as a direct address to the addressee(s)—the "peoples" subject to Europe's princes—urging collective resistance to the suppression of freedom of thought (*Denkfreiheit*).[^1] [^2] It is translated in the English version as "peoples" or "people," without strong connotations of ethnic *nation* (as in Fichte's later nationalist phase, e.g., *Reden an die deutsche Nation*). Instead, *Volk* functions here in a broadly republican-enlightenment sense: the mass of rights-bearing individuals who, via the social contract, have delegated executive power (*ausübende Gewalt*) to princes but retain inalienable rights, especially to free thought and communication.[^3] [^4]

The pamphlet is framed as a speech "reclaiming" (*zurückforderung*) freedom of thought *from* (*von*) princes (*Fürsten*), who are accused of abusing delegated authority to suppress enlightenment. The *Volk* enters not as a passive object but as an active moral force: Fichte rallies "you peoples" (*ihr Völker*) to prioritize *Denkfreiheit* above all sacrifices (wars, taxes, etc.), warning that cowardice risks future generations' vengeance and regression to "spiritual despotism."[^1] Princes are critiqued as often ignorant or manipulated (*Satelliten des Despotismus*), with the *Volk* positioned as wiser and stronger in aggregate, capable of revolutions if enlightenment is blocked.[^5]

Later, *Volk* recurs in critiques of princely manipulation: princes allegedly keep their "people" (*Volk*, *Völker*) in intellectual darkness to maintain control, fostering blind habituation to state-sanctioned "truths" rather than genuine conviction.[^6] [^7] True advisors urge princes to enlighten "your peoples" (*eure Völker*), as light would affirm (not undermine) legitimate rule.[^8] The *Volk* thus embodies humanity's progressive destiny, contrasting princely "barbarism" and error-prone "world-government" ambitions.[^9]

The corpus does not exhaustively index every *Volk* instance (English searches yield fewer hits due to translation variance), but it is integral: without invoking the *Volk*, the pamphlet's contractarian demand for princes to protect (not infringe) rights lacks force.

[^1]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, [paragraph](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/01-preface/original/#p-028daa).

[^2]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, [paragraph](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/01-preface/translation/#p-20dba4).

[^3]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, ch. 2, [full chapter](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02-speech/translation/).

[^4]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, [paragraph](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02-speech/original/#p-04aa90).

[^5]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, ch. 1, [full chapter](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/01-preface/translation/).

[^6]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, [paragraph](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02-speech/original/#p-9c779f).

[^7]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, [paragraph](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02-speech/translation/#p-28e050).

[^8]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, [paragraph](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02-speech/original/#p-65248c).

[^9]: Johann Gottlieb Fichte, *Zurückforderung der Denkfreiheit von den Fürsten Europens*, [paragraph](/works/johann-gottlieb-fichte-zuruckforderung-der-denkfreiheit-bookde/02-speech/original/#p-5cf219).
